results.description: This document provides a guide to instruments and methods for measuring various meteorological variables. It contains 17 chapters describing the measurement of variables such as temperature, pressure, humidity, wind, precipitation, radiation, ...
results.content:
This document provides a guide to instruments and methods for measuring various meteorological variables. It contains 17 chapters describing the measurement of variables such as temperature, pressure, humidity, wind, precipitation, radiation, ...
This document provides a guide to instruments and methods for measuring various meteorological variables. It contains 17 chapters describing the measurement of variables such as temperature, pressure, humidity, wind, precipitation, radiation, and others. The guide is intended to provide recommended practices and guidelines for measuring these variables rather than detailed instruction manuals.

Metadata (data about data) should be kept concerning all of the station establishment and maintenance matters described in section 1.3, and concerning changes which occur, including calibra-tion and maintenance history and the changes in terms of exposure and staff (WMO, 2003b). Metadata are especially important for elements which are particularly sensitive to exposure, such as precipita-tion, wind and temperature. One very basic form of metadata is information on the existence, availabil-ity and quality of meteorological data and of the metadata about them. 1.2 Meteorological observing systems The requirements for observational data may be met using in situ measurements or remote-sens-ing (including space-borne) systems, according to the ability of the various sensing systems to meas-ure the elements needed.
Chapter 1. GENERALI.1–3 Atmospheric pressure (Chapter 3) Precipitation (Chapter 6) Snow cover (Chapter 6) Sunshine and/ or solar radiation (Chapters 7, 8) Soil temperature (Chapter 2) Evaporation (Chapter 10) Instruments exist which can measure all of these elements, except cloud type. However, with current technology, instruments for present and past weather, cloud amount and height, and snow cover are not able to make observations of the whole range of phenomena, whereas human observers are able to do so.

WMO-NO.08 volume IV edisi 2021 - Free download as PDF File (.pdf), Text File (.txt) or read online for free. This document provides guidance on instruments and methods for observing systems at automatic weather stations. It details the general concepts, system configurations, hardware, software, siting considerations, maintenance and cost analysis for automatic weather stations.
All-in-one AWSs are also available, designed by several suppliers of professional meteorological equipment. They include a set of embedded sensors with adapted electronics and software. Price, compactness and ease of installation are the advantages of these all-in-one AWSs, usually allowing the measurement of wind (with an ultrasonic instrument), air temperature and relative humidity within an embedded radiation screen, pressure and precipitation (by radar, detection of droplet hits, or with a more classical tipping bucket raingauge at the top of the instrument).
Commission for Instruments and Methods of Observation (CIMO) gives the following advice to Members of WMO and organizations taking meteorological measurements.

results.question: How do manual visual observations complement automated systems in Marine Weather Observation?
results.answer: Manual visual observations complement automated systems in Marine Weather Observations by filling in observational gaps where automated systems might struggle. For instance, in conditions where sensor readings might be compromised, such as low wind detection near land due to tide or swell, visual methods help determine wind speed and direction through qualitative indicators such as foam streaks or ships' smoke . Manual observations are useful at night or in visual-obstructed areas, where direct sensor measurements from waves might not be feasible. This dual approach ensures more thorough data

results.question: What are the key design considerations for selecting radar systems for specific meteorological applications, such as snow squall detection or tornado monitoring?
results.answer: Key design considerations for radar systems in specific meteorological applications focus on optimizing radar characteristics to best meet the application's needs. For snow squall detection, short-range X-band radars are advantageous due to their small wavelength and ability to provide high-resolution data. These radars must have low-level coverage and be capable of adaptive sensing for critical applications . In contrast, tornado monitoring requires radars with high Doppler capabilities and high pulse repetition frequencies (PRF) to accurately measure wind velocities and detect rotational sig

results.question: What are the challenges of integrating Wind Measurements into Automatic Weather Stations (AWS) and maintaining data accuracy?
results.answer: Integrating wind measurements into AWS presents several challenges. First, AWS must handle high-frequency sampling rates to derive wind parameters efficiently, which can be difficult with data loggers that may not support such requirements, as mentioned in Source 2. Moreover, wind sensors like anemometers and vanes often face mechanical wear in harsh environments, necessitating regular maintenance and calibration to maintain data accuracy . Saltwater exposure on buoys, for example, can lead to mechanical failures unless mitigated through the use of ultrasonic anemometers, which have fewer movi

results.question: How do marine weather instruments adapt to the challenges associated with sea conditions in measuring environmental variables?
results.answer: Marine weather instruments are designed to adapt to harsh sea conditions through robust hardware and specific technologies. For wind measurement, instruments like ultrasonic anemometers are preferred due to their durability and lack of moving parts, which reduce maintenance needs in the corrosive marine environment . Measurements of atmospheric pressure are typically performed using digital barometers that account for differences between sea-level and station pressure, crucial for accurate weather forecasting . To measure wave conditions, buoys are equipped with sensors for wave energy, period

results.question: What are the potential sources of error in wireless radar measurements of precipitation, and how can these errors be mitigated?
results.answer: Potential sources of error in wireless radar measurements of precipitation include radar beam filling, which occurs especially at long ranges where the pulse volume may not be completely filled with homogeneous precipitation, leading to significant smoothing . Beam blocking by topography or obstacles can also result in underestimations as certain areas may be occluded . Attenuation of radar signals by precipitation, particularly rain, can diminish the signal's strength and lead to inaccuracies . Non-uniformity in the vertical distribution of precipitation affects the accuracy of ground-level p

results.question: Why is real-time quality control critical for Automatic Weather Stations data accuracy, and how is it implemented?
results.answer: Real-time quality control is critical for AWS data accuracy as it helps minimize inaccuracies and missing data by immediately identifying and correcting errors in the field, thus maintaining the integrity of weather observations . Quality control is implemented through procedures such as hardware self-checks, which monitor the performance of instruments, and software routines that perform data-quality checks and isolate spurious errors. These measures facilitate continuous monitoring and allow for immediate corrective actions, reducing downtime and maintaining high data quality . Remote diagno
results.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

results.question: What role does the coding of standard messages at a central level play in the transmission of meteorological data, and how does it address AWS-specific challenges?
results.answer: The coding of standard messages at a central level in meteorological data transmission plays a pivotal role by ensuring that the transmitted variables remain uniform and compatible with the global telecommunications system, such as the WMO's BUFR tables . This standardization allows for seamless data exchange and reduces the need for changes within the transmission layer when upgrades occur. AWS-specific challenges, like the transmission of service data from unique AWS designs, are addressed by coding these additional service parameters into the central message system, which ensures consistent

results.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of using ultrasonic anemometers on marine buoys for wind measurement?
results.answer: Ultrasonic anemometers have several advantages when used on marine buoys. They contain no moving parts, which reduces the maintenance frequency and the risk of mechanical failure due to environmental exposure, such as saltwater corrosion and crystal formation in traditional mechanical components . This makes them more reliable for long-term deployments in harsh maritime environments. However, the disadvantages include potentially higher costs associated with acquiring and deploying the ultrasonic technology compared to traditional anemometers. Additionally, any electronic malfunctions could re
results.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

results.question: How does the concept of modularity influence the design and operation of Automatic Weather Stations?
results.answer: Modularity significantly influences the design and operation of AWS by allowing adaptability to new instruments, variables, and changes in telecommunications networks . It provides the flexibility needed to replace or upgrade instruments without major redesigns, facilitating easier maintenance and system upgrades, and potentially extending the AWS's operational life. However, increased modularity can lead to higher initial costs because of the more complex design and wider range of components required to accommodate different setups . Therefore, designing with modularity involves balancing fle

results.question: In what ways does the use of time of arrival (TOA) technique in LINET contribute to its effectiveness in lightning detection?
results.answer: The use of the time of arrival (TOA) technique in LINET enhances its effectiveness by providing precise location data for lightning events. TOA allows LINET to determine the height and classification of lightning activities by measuring the time it takes for a signal to reach various sensors, which are typically spaced within a certain distance range . This capability enables effective differentiation between cloud-to-ground (CG) strokes and in-cloud processes, improving the network's detection accuracy. items.question: What are the challenges of integrating Wind Measurements into Automatic Weather Stations (AWS) and maintaining data accuracy?
items.answer: Integrating wind measurements into AWS presents several challenges. First, AWS must handle high-frequency sampling rates to derive wind parameters efficiently, which can be difficult with data loggers that may not support such requirements, as mentioned in Source 2. Moreover, wind sensors like anemometers and vanes often face mechanical wear in harsh environments, necessitating regular maintenance and calibration to maintain data accuracy . Saltwater exposure on buoys, for example, can lead to mechanical failures unless mitigated through the use of ultrasonic anemometers, which have fewer moving parts and thus require less maintenance . Additionally, ensuring that wind measurement data remain consistent and free from spurious errors demands strict adherence to quality control protocols, utilizing robust hardware and software routines . Incorporating these systems effectively into an AWS framework requires careful consideration of maintenance costs and the potential need for remote diagnostics to ensure consistent data integrity.
items.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

items.question: What are the potential sources of error in wireless radar measurements of precipitation, and how can these errors be mitigated?
items.answer:
Potential sources of error in wireless radar measurements of precipitation include radar beam filling, which occurs especially at long ranges where the pulse volume may not be completely filled with homogeneous precipitation, leading to significant smoothing . Beam blocking by topography or obstacles can also result in underestimations as certain areas may be occluded . Attenuation of radar signals by precipitation, particularly rain, can diminish the signal's strength and lead to inaccuracies . Non-uniformity in the vertical distribution of precipitation affects the accuracy of ground-level precipitation estimates due to variations in precipitation intensity as a function of height . 

Mitigation strategies include reorientation or re-siting of radar installations to avoid wind turbine interference, which can create false echoes through multiple reflections and multi-path scattering . The use of dual-polarization radar techniques helps compensate for attenuation errors, as it enables the detection of the specific differential phase (KDP), which is less affected by attenuation and provides cleaner and more accurate measurements . For beam blockage, data interpolation can be utilized to fill gaps in quantitative applications, while improving data processing and calibration can correct systemic biases and enhance the quality of radar data ."}
items.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

items.question: How do marine weather instruments adapt to the challenges associated with sea conditions in measuring environmental variables?
items.answer: Marine weather instruments are designed to adapt to harsh sea conditions through robust hardware and specific technologies. For wind measurement, instruments like ultrasonic anemometers are preferred due to their durability and lack of moving parts, which reduce maintenance needs in the corrosive marine environment . Measurements of atmospheric pressure are typically performed using digital barometers that account for differences between sea-level and station pressure, crucial for accurate weather forecasting . To measure wave conditions, buoys are equipped with sensors for wave energy, period, and height, developed to withstand the continuous motion and salty conditions in open waters . These adaptations ensure reliable data collection despite the difficult environmental conditions encountered at sea.
items.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

items.question: What are the key design considerations for selecting radar systems for specific meteorological applications, such as snow squall detection or tornado monitoring?
items.answer: Key design considerations for radar systems in specific meteorological applications focus on optimizing radar characteristics to best meet the application's needs. For snow squall detection, short-range X-band radars are advantageous due to their small wavelength and ability to provide high-resolution data. These radars must have low-level coverage and be capable of adaptive sensing for critical applications . In contrast, tornado monitoring requires radars with high Doppler capabilities and high pulse repetition frequencies (PRF) to accurately measure wind velocities and detect rotational signatures inherent to tornado formation . The radar network design must consider the range, ensuring overlapping coverage to mitigate any single system's blind spots, and infrastructure must support phased arrays for efficient scanning in complex terrains. Additionally, these systems must balance cost against performance, exploring modularity to allow future upgrades without excessive retooling .
items.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

items.question: In what ways does the use of time of arrival (TOA) technique in LINET contribute to its effectiveness in lightning detection?
items.answer: The use of the time of arrival (TOA) technique in LINET enhances its effectiveness by providing precise location data for lightning events. TOA allows LINET to determine the height and classification of lightning activities by measuring the time it takes for a signal to reach various sensors, which are typically spaced within a certain distance range . This capability enables effective differentiation between cloud-to-ground (CG) strokes and in-cloud processes, improving the network's detection accuracy. The proximity of sensors and the TOA method's sensitivity to low-amplitude signals also allow for effective mapping of lightning channels and processes, aiding in applications like severe weather tracking . However, it is crucial for sensors to be within 100 km of the lightning discharge to achieve reliable differentiation, highlighting the importance of network density .
items.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

items.question: How do manual visual observations complement automated systems in Marine Weather Observation?
items.answer: Manual visual observations complement automated systems in Marine Weather Observations by filling in observational gaps where automated systems might struggle. For instance, in conditions where sensor readings might be compromised, such as low wind detection near land due to tide or swell, visual methods help determine wind speed and direction through qualitative indicators such as foam streaks or ships' smoke . Manual observations are useful at night or in visual-obstructed areas, where direct sensor measurements from waves might not be feasible. This dual approach ensures more thorough data collection and validation, enhancing the reliability of the weather analysis .

items.question: How does the concept of modularity influence the design and operation of Automatic Weather Stations?
items.answer: Modularity significantly influences the design and operation of AWS by allowing adaptability to new instruments, variables, and changes in telecommunications networks . It provides the flexibility needed to replace or upgrade instruments without major redesigns, facilitating easier maintenance and system upgrades, and potentially extending the AWS's operational life. However, increased modularity can lead to higher initial costs because of the more complex design and wider range of components required to accommodate different setups . Therefore, designing with modularity involves balancing flexibility and cost-efficiency while anticipating future changes.

items.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of using ultrasonic anemometers on marine buoys for wind measurement?
items.answer: Ultrasonic anemometers have several advantages when used on marine buoys. They contain no moving parts, which reduces the maintenance frequency and the risk of mechanical failure due to environmental exposure, such as saltwater corrosion and crystal formation in traditional mechanical components . This makes them more reliable for long-term deployments in harsh maritime environments. However, the disadvantages include potentially higher costs associated with acquiring and deploying the ultrasonic technology compared to traditional anemometers. Additionally, any electronic malfunctions could require specialized repairs, which may not be as straightforward as mechanical fixes in traditional systems .
items.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

items.question: What role does the coding of standard messages at a central level play in the transmission of meteorological data, and how does it address AWS-specific challenges?
items.answer: The coding of standard messages at a central level in meteorological data transmission plays a pivotal role by ensuring that the transmitted variables remain uniform and compatible with the global telecommunications system, such as the WMO's BUFR tables . This standardization allows for seamless data exchange and reduces the need for changes within the transmission layer when upgrades occur. AWS-specific challenges, like the transmission of service data from unique AWS designs, are addressed by coding these additional service parameters into the central message system, which ensures consistent communication and reduces local processing requirements . Centralized coding thus enhances data reliability and compatibility across diverse systems and use cases.
items.title: WMO No. 8: Instruments & Observation Methods | PDF | Meteorology ...

items.question: Why is real-time quality control critical for Automatic Weather Stations data accuracy, and how is it implemented?
items.answer: Real-time quality control is critical for AWS data accuracy as it helps minimize inaccuracies and missing data by immediately identifying and correcting errors in the field, thus maintaining the integrity of weather observations . Quality control is implemented through procedures such as hardware self-checks, which monitor the performance of instruments, and software routines that perform data-quality checks and isolate spurious errors. These measures facilitate continuous monitoring and allow for immediate corrective actions, reducing downtime and maintaining high data quality . Remote diagnostic capabilities augment these procedures by enabling maintenance personnel to perform checkups and recalibrations from afar, significantly reducing the time and cost associated with field operations .

extra_snippets: WMO-NO.08 volume IV edisi 2021 - Free download as PDF File (.pdf), Text File (.txt) or read online for free. This document provides guidance on instruments and methods for observing systems at automatic weather stations. It details the general concepts, system configurations, hardware, software, siting considerations, maintenance and cost analysis for automatic weather stations.

All-in-one AWSs are also available, designed by several suppliers of professional meteorological equipment. They include a set of embedded sensors with adapted electronics and software. Price, compactness and ease of installation are the advantages of these all-in-one AWSs, usually allowing the measurement of wind (with an ultrasonic instrument), air temperature and relative humidity within an embedded radiation screen, pressure and precipitation (by radar, detection of droplet hits, or with a more classical tipping bucket raingauge at the top of the instrument).
Commission for Instruments and Methods of Observation (CIMO) gives the following advice to Members of WMO and organizations taking meteorological measurements.

(b) “Basic” AWSs for the measurement of “basic” meteorological measurements (typically air temperature, relative humidity, wind speed and direction, precipitation and, sometimes, atmospheric pressure); (c) “Extended” AWSs with the additional measurement of solar radiation, sunshine duration, soil temperature, evaporation and so forth; (d) AWSs with automation of visual observations: basic or extended AWSs with automatic observation of visibility, cloud-base height and present weather.

Therefore it is not a fixed quality of any observation, but results from joint appraisal of instrumentation, measurement interval and exposure against the requirements of some particular application.
